mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with falling inflation indicates healthy demand without price pressures, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >58% of periods where INDPRO declines during tightening, or the regime filter adds no incremental IC over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.3 million units, construction sector weakness cascades into broader consumer spending declines, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where HOUST drops below 1.3M, or the threshold shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates driven by high nominal rates and low inflation compress equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the combined proxy fails to outperform single-series models in out-of-sample IC.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a post-recession recovery phase boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >57% of periods where GDPC1 growth accelerates during recovery, or the signal loses significance outside the specified regime.
